entities.name: Astaroth
entities.raw_quote: A few of the experiments list demons whose names are familiar from other sources: Astaroth, Baruch, Belial, Belzebub, Berith, Castiel, Lucifer, Mirael, Paymon and Satan or Sathan.

entities.name: Baruch
entities.raw_quote: A few of the experiments list demons whose names are familiar from other sources: Astaroth, Baruch, Belial, Belzebub, Berith, Castiel, Lucifer, Mirael, Paymon and Satan or Sathan.

entities.name: Belial
entities.raw_quote: Bartha is a king; Baltim, Galtim, and Saltim are dukes; Belial is ‘foremost prince’

entities.name: Orient
entities.raw_quote: In two experiments the spirit addressed is named Orient... 'O most high and benign king Orient', 'O benign Orient, maker of the greater part of the world, heaven, and earth'

entities.name: Apolin
entities.raw_quote: ...one conjures not only Belzebub, Berith, Lucifer and Sathan, but also Apolin, Beliath and Maraloch

entities.name: Belferith

entities.name: Altramat
entities.raw_quote: ...a third gives the altogether well established name Satan (spelled ‘Sathan’) but also the exceptional ones Altramat, Archidemath, Discobermath, Fritath, Hegergibet, Helyberp, Pestiferat and Sona

entities.name: Curson
entities.raw_quote: When the list of spirits in this manual is compared with that in Picatrix... Of the eleven spirits named in experiment no. 34, only Curson has broad currency

entities.name: Hanni
entities.raw_quote: ...only Curson has broad currency (unless Hanni is equated with Haniel)

entities.name: Iamblichus
entities.raw_quote: The notion of a demonic hierarchy... stems chiefly from the Neoplatonists, and above all Iamblichus.

entities.name: Proclus

attributes.role: Neoplatonist who categorized daimones by elements

entities.raw_quote: Proclus, drawing on Iamblichus’s elaborate categorization, spoke of daimones as ruling the fire, the air, the water, the earth and the underground region

entities.name: Michael Psellus
entities.raw_quote: ...to this system Psellus added lucifugues (or light-fleeing) demons

entities.name: Johannes Trithemius

attributes.role: lent authority to the expanded demon hierarchy

entities.raw_quote: ...and Johannes Trithemius lent the weight of his authority to this system

entities.name: Thomas Aquinas
entities.raw_quote: ...a ‘Lowerarchy’, as C.S. Lewis called it... The notion of a demonic hierarchy... is an assumption that the Malleus maleficarum shared with Thomas Aquinas.

entities.name: C.S. Lewis
entities.raw_quote: ...a ‘Lowerarchy’, as C.S. Lewis called it

entities.name: Apuleius

attributes.role: source of daimon concept in Christian tradition

entities.raw_quote: Apuleius’s De deo Socratis remained long influential... daemones were rational beings whose natural sphere was the sublunary air

entities.name: John of Frankfurt
entities.raw_quote: John of Frankfurt devoted a treatise in 1412 to the question... whether it is possible to have the power of coercing a demon through characters, figures, and recitation of words

entities.name: Nicholas Eymericus

attributes.role: theologian who described demonic honor as sacrifice

entities.raw_quote: Nicholas Eymericus explains that some conjurers exhibit the honour of latria to the demons they invoke by sacrificing to them...

entities.name: Munich Handbook (CLM 849)
entities.raw_quote: Altogether 189 spirits are addressed by name in the Munich handbook’s conjurations

entities.name: Picatrix
entities.raw_quote: When the list of spirits in this manual is compared with that in Picatrix, what is striking is the singular lack of correspondence.

entities.name: Malleus maleficarum
entities.raw_quote: ...the Malleus maleficarum shared with Thomas Aquinas.

entities.name: Rawlinson MS D 252
entities.raw_quote: Compare the wording of the conjuration in Bodleian Library MS Rawlinson D 252...
